It's gonna be a great one today. I have such great stuff to talk about. Causing some stuff that's going on in my own business, which is really crystallized the difference between marketing and sales. And I really think this is important for me to explain to you because I see so much you know, these days about what is sales and about sales is not really selling anymore because of the internet and it's a new way of selling it. It's all such a load of horseshit. And I really need to really clarify this to you because it's as if people are thinking there's some sort of shortcut to no longer having to become an influencer through your voice, meaning in person either or over the phone, communicating as a human being. You can just do it all over the internet. And it's just an absolute bunch of, so let me explain, all right? It starts off with this, and the reason this is important today just, you know, is because I want you to understand how crucial the things I'm teaching you are to your success, whether you're a salesperson or a marketer or an entrepreneur. In other words, this is weird perception out there that something has changed now with the internet and you no longer have to master the art of persuasion, meaning talking to a person or one on 10, one on a thousand that you can use the internet to circumvent all that. It's not true, it's a lie. And you would think that since, as you know, now I am using the internet in a way I've never used before, I'm blasting, I'm smashing it, all right? I'll tell you the truth. I'm running Facebook campaigns now, and I'm getting higher conversion rates than anybody in history on call traffic. No one's done what I've done. I'm making money on call traffic, and it's because they have a movie behind me and everyone knows who I am pretty much around the world and the straight lines very well established, so I'm lucky like that, right? But what I do on my landing pages and the way I use my straight line system in terms of language patterns and my looping, right? That falls into the category of marketing. It's not sales, all right? And you know, nothing has changed, you know, the only difference to the online world and the offline world is the online world has these great advantages for reaching people quickly, for getting data really quickly, for being able to essentially close someone on a page up to a certain amount of money, but that's no different than the old fashioned infomercials, like there's really no different than what's happening right now when you have a funnel going on, than when you see an infomercial on TV. They're doing the same thing that you do on a landing page. They spend 30 minutes or 15 minutes in looping around and going through a certain product or a service or back when Tony Robbins used to do his, like you know, 20 years ago on TV, right? It was all the same. The point is, is that at a certain level, you cannot use the internet to get you the bigger sales. You're capped out. I'm not gonna delude myself into thinking I could sell a $2,000 product in mass by simply having a landing pages. At a certain point in time, if you wanna get those higher level programs, if you wanna sell things at higher price points, for the most part, like 99% of the time, you have to speak to a human being. Now, like for instance, I see a company right now called Carvana, right? You see this new commercial, Carvana, where they're trying to sell people cars over the internet. Where you just pick up a car and you're buying it online and so forth, right? Well, let me say this. Number one, what they're doing is, they're selling commodity items. They're selling cars that people know about that are essentially, they're using very price point specific items that people are very familiar with, right? And they're trying to use sort of like an auction system online, you look, you can see your car, right? With no sales person involved, okay? Different model. The point I'm making here is that if you think that the person who, let's say has a used car a lot, okay? When someone walks on that lot or into a dealership, okay? You're gonna get a much higher closing rate by having that person, a human being there, to actually close the deal, then through a website. You just are. Websites are, so, I'm Carvana, right? At a certain point in time, yeah, they can go so far with that, but opposite, I promise you, at a certain point, they will have human interaction into the equation, okay? To try to increase the closing rate, to try to get more sales, okay? And I don't know the model that well, but what I do know is that this notion that you can just master the internet and sit the home and not ever see anyone in person or use your voice on the phone is nonsense. You can't master marketing. That's all marketing, it's not selling. In other words, you become a master marketer, right? Yeah, you can develop great landing pages, you know, do great Facebook campaigns. In fact, I'm doing so much of it right now. I'm gonna be teaching a Facebook course very soon, okay? I'm gonna be helping a whole full-blown Facebook course and on my marketing course, right? Because I'm becoming an expert at it really, really fast. But that's marketing, that's not selling. I can only go so far with that. Yeah, I'll get people to sign up for $9.95 a month, and I'm giving them the most ridiculous value possible there, right? I give an offer that people can't refuse, which is why it's doing so well. But at the end of the day, if I wanna sell my flagship program for $2,000, I'm gonna have to talk to someone, bottom line, all right? Or if I wanna even, in probably $2,000, you can get to that number. But if I wanna go to the coaching programs, but when I charge 100 grand a year, or actually a month, okay, or a million dollars a year, or I wanna have a coaching program, other coaches working for me, and they charge 10 grand a month, right? I'm gonna have to have people closing them, getting on the phone, and using influence and persuasion. You understand? Marketing takes you so far. It's a crucial aspect of selling. I always say that, marketing's on one side, sales on the other. But don't delude yourself in any way. Thinking is a shortcut, a way out of just becoming a master internet marketer, and you'll never have to speak to anybody again. Influence cuts to every aspect of life and business. So if you're an internet marketer, you're gonna have to get people to come work for you. That's influence and persuasion. Get people to negotiate salaries. You have to talk to your credit card process, to get a good deal there. You have to talk to your landlord, who you get space from. It just goes on, or if you ever wanna get into the really higher price points, you have to get the phone and close people.
